input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
Here's what you need:

## 1. Add a `Directory.Build.props` (at solution root)

This sets properties consistently across all test projects:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <UseTestingPlatform>true</UseTestingPlatform>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

- **`UseTestingPlatform`** — switches MSTest 3.8+ to emit a Microsoft.Testing.Platform executable instead of a VSTest adapter.
- **`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`** — enables the `dotnet test` integration so CI doesn't break (MTP apps are standalone by default).

## 2. Per-project changes

No `.csproj` edits required beyond what's in `Directory.Build.props`, **provided** your projects already reference `MSTest` 3.8.0 (the unified package). If they still reference the older split packages, replace:

```xml
<!-- Remove these -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.0" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="..." />

<!-- Replace with -->
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
```

`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` is no longer needed — MTP hosts the tests directly.

## 3. CI command change

```bash
# Before
dotnet test --logger trx

# After
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory ./TestResults
```

The command itself still works (thanks to `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`). The TRX logger is built into MTP, but you should add the extension package if it's not already pulled in by MSTest 3.8:

```xml
<!-- In Directory.Build.props or Directory.Packages.props -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.*" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## Summary of what each property does

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `UseTestingPlatform` | Builds test project as MTP host (no VSTest) |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Keeps `dotnet test` working (bridges to MTP) |

No other changes are needed — MSTest 3.8 already bundles the MTP test framework and runner.
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to migrate 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). They're on .NET 8 SDK and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI.

Let me provide a concise answer covering the changes needed.
